name: Georgia HOPE Scholarship
description: Georgia's primary state scholarship program for residents who demonstrate academic achievement. HOPE (Helping Outstanding Pupils Educationally) includes HOPE Scholarship (merit-based), HOPE Grant (for career/vocational students), and Zell Miller Scholarship (top academic performers).
eligibility: Georgia resident, U.S. citizen or eligible non-citizen, graduated from a Georgia eligible high school with required GPA (3.0 for HOPE, 3.7 for Zell Miller), and meetingrigorous curriculum requirements. Must be enrolled at eligible Georgia public or private college.
award_amount: HOPE Scholarship: covers a set tuition rate per credit hour at Georgia public colleges (approximately 90-100% of tuition depending on rate changes); private college award is $4,000/year. Zell Miller Scholarship: full tuition plus mandatory fees at Georgia public colleges.
application_process: File GSFICS application online through Georgia Student Finance Commission. Georgia colleges receive data automatically for eligible students. Must be a Georgia resident for tuition purposes (one year before enrollment).
timeline: Apply at beginning of senior year. Initial eligibility determined after high school graduation. Renewed annually based on GPA (3.0 minimum for HOPE, 3.3 for Zell Miller at 30 attempted hours).
advisor_notes: HOPE GPA is recalculated at each academic milestone (30, 60, 90 credit hours). Students who start with 3.0 must maintain it — many lose HOPE after 30 hours. Zell Miller recipients must maintain 3.3. Check Hope GPA rules — grade forgiveness policies can help. HOPE covers tuition only at public colleges.
stacking_notes: HOPE stacks with Pell Grant in most cases. However, some private colleges may reduce institutional aid when combined with HOPE. HOPE does not cover fees — students should budget for those separately. Zell Miller is more generous but has stricter GPA requirements. Federal SEOG may reduce the self-help portion of an aid package.
